messages.content: I need a user login feature with email and password, return a token on success

plan.description: Create user model and database schema for storing user credentials (email, password hash).

interface.name: createUserModel
interface.description: Creates and returns a Sequelize model for User with fields: id, email, passwordHash, createdAt, updatedAt. The email field should be unique and validated for presence.

plan.description: Implement email format validation utility function.

interface.name: validateEmailFormat
interface.description: Validates that the provided string matches a standard email format using regex. Returns true if valid, false otherwise.

plan.description: Create password hashing and verification utility functions.

interface.name: verifyPassword
interface.description: Compares a plain text password with a hashed password using bcrypt. Returns a promise that resolves to true if they match, false otherwise.

plan.description: Implement JWT token generation and configuration (secret, expiration).

interface.name: generateJWT
interface.description: Generates a JSON Web Token (JWT) using the provided payload (e.g., userId, email). Uses a secret key from environment variables and sets an expiration (e.g., 24h).

plan.description: Create login route handler with input validation (email format check).

interface.name: loginRouteHandler
interface.description: Express route handler for POST /api/auth/login. Validates request body for email and password fields, checks email format, and passes control to the auth controller.

plan.description: Implement credential verification logic (email lookup, password hash match).

interface.name: verifyCredentials
interface.description: Looks up a user by email in the database, verifies the password hash matches, and returns the user object (excluding passwordHash) if valid. Returns null if user not found or password mismatch.

plan.description: Add response handling for success (200 with token) and error cases (400, 401).

interface.name: handleLoginResponse
interface.description: Handles the response for login attempts. If user is valid, generates a JWT and sends 200 with token. If invalid credentials, sends 401. If missing fields or invalid email, sends 400.

plan.description: Write integration tests for login endpoint covering all acceptance criteria.

interface.name: runLoginIntegrationTests
interface.description: Runs integration tests for the login endpoint using a test framework (e.g., Jest/Supertest). Tests include: valid login returns token, invalid email format returns 400, wrong password returns 401, non-existent email returns 401.

requirements.title: User Login with Email and Password
requirements.description: Implement a user authentication system that allows users to log in using their registered email address and password. Upon successful authentication, the system must generate and return a secure authentication token.
acceptance_criteria: User can submit login credentials via email and password fields
System validates email format before processing authentication
System verifies password matches the stored hash for the provided email
System returns a 200 OK response with a valid JWT token upon successful authentication
System returns a 401 Unauthorized response for invalid credentials
System returns a 400 Bad Request response for malformed email format
Generated token includes user ID and has a configurable expiration time
Token is returned in the response body as JSON (e.g., {"token": "eyJhbGciOiJ..."})
